show_of_the_month.title: Doron Langberg
show_of_the_month.short_description: The artist reflects on personal and historical trauma through a series of paintings influenced by experiences in Israel, Ukraine, and New York, exploring themes of home, loss, and justice amidst conflict.

top_5_seen.title: Doron Langberg
top_5_seen.short_description: The artist reflects on personal and historical trauma through a series of paintings influenced by experiences in Israel, Ukraine, and New York, exploring themes of home, loss, and justice amidst conflict.

top_5_seen.title: Daniel Arsham
top_5_seen.short_description: Perrotin New York presents "Various Thoughts," highlighting Daniel Arsham's exploration of memory and architecture through sculptures and paintings that blend artifact and machine, permanence and loss. The exhibition features interactive elements like sonic sculptures, inviting viewers to engage with the fusion of past and future. Arsham's multidisciplinary approach transcends traditional boundaries, reflecting on the interplay between decay and renewal in art.

top_5_seen.title: Outsider Art Fair
top_5_seen.short_description: The Outsider Art Fair returns to New York City for its 34th edition at the Metropolitan Pavilion, showcasing self-taught artists and fostering global discussions. VIP preview on March 19. Tickets available online.

top_5_seen.title: Cynthia Daignault
top_5_seen.short_description: Olney Gleason presents "Denali," an exhibition by Cynthia Daignault from February 19 to March 28, 2026, featuring twelve new paintings that explore the Alaskan mountain's themes of beauty, loss, and climate change.

top_5_seen.title: futures of repair
top_5_seen.short_description: The "Futures of Repair" exhibition invites attendees to explore a transformed world centered on healing and reparations through immersive art, workshops, and community engagement, running July 25, 2025, to Sunday evenings.

top_5_saved.title: Peter Saul
top_5_saved.short_description: Peter Saul's inaugural solo exhibition at Gladstone Gallery features 34 new and historic works, exploring his reinterpretations of 20th-century art, including a new take on Picasso's Guernica. The exhibition opens March 7 in New York. Saul's vibrant, satirical approach challenges conventions while engaging with art historical themes.

top_5_saved.title: Doron Langberg
top_5_saved.short_description: The artist reflects on personal and historical trauma through a series of paintings influenced by experiences in Israel, Ukraine, and New York, exploring themes of home, loss, and justice amidst conflict.

top_5_saved.title: Glenn Ligon
top_5_saved.short_description: Glenn Ligon's exhibition, "Late at night, early in the morning, at noon," features new and historic works exploring language, abstraction, and the color blue, reflecting on emotional and cultural themes through text and image. The show combines large-scale new works and prints spanning three decades, highlighting Ligon's evolution in printmaking and engagement with American history and literature. By intertwining Baldwin's writings with visual art, Ligon evokes deeper emotional truths and historical reflections.

top_5_saved.title: studio visit: a curatorial project by anicka yi and josh kline for performance space new york
top_5_saved.short_description: Hauser & Wirth presents "Studio Visit," an exhibition in partnership with Performance Space New York, exploring the 21st-century artist's studio as a site of collaboration, identity, and community.

top_5_saved.title: Chris Wiley
top_5_saved.short_description: Emily Ferguson, Tomas Harker, and Sol Kordich present diverse artistic explorations of identity and emotion through figuration, mediated imagery, and abstraction in a dynamic visual landscape.

top_5_views.title: analog: 30th anniversary exhibition
top_5_views.short_description: The upcoming exhibition at Anton Kern Gallery, titled "Analog," showcases the artist's fascination with vibrant, messy artwork and long-held friendships, featuring notable artists and a unique VHS collection giveaway.

top_5_views.short_description: Perrotin New York presents "Various Thoughts," highlighting Daniel Arsham's exploration of memory and architecture through sculptures and paintings that blend artifact and machine, permanence and loss. Arsham's multidisciplinary approach transcends traditional boundaries, reflecting on the interplay between decay and renewal in art.

The show combines large-scale new works and prints spanning three decades, highlighting Ligon's evolution in printmaking and engagement with American history and literature. top_5_views.title: traces of motion
top_5_views.short_description: A group exhibition features artists Gianluca Bianchino, Janos Korodi, and Lisa Lackey, examining landscapes as complex intersections of human experience, technology, and natural systems. Their diverse works invite reflection on our impact on the world.
